business.headline: RBI's latest worry isn't inflation. It's artificial intelligenceArtificial intelligence is no longer just transforming banking—it is emerging as one of the biggest threats to the financial system. In its latest Financial Stability Report, the RBI has warned that AI-powered cyberattacks, dependence on common AI platforms and growing technology risks could pose fresh challenges for banks and financial institutions.

IndiGo's new budget-friendly 'Lite' fare for cabin baggage flyers takes off July 15According to the airline, the fare is designed for passengers travelling with only cabin baggage. By paying only for the services they use, customers can have greater control over their travel costs.

A rare NRI investment opportunity, lost in paperwork? Nithin Kamath thinks soThe Zerodha co-founder says lengthy onboarding processes may prevent NRIs from taking advantage of attractive FCNR deposit rates enabled by a recent RBI move.

8th Pay Commission: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 69,000? Minimum pay debate explainedEmployee unions and staff bodies have asked the 8th Pay Commission to raise the minimum basic pay. The debate has widened into a question of inflation, family needs and a fair living wage.

7 big financial changes from July 1 every Indian must knowJuly has brought several important financial changes that could affect your everyday life. From ITR filing deadlines and passport fees to Aadhaar updates, banking rules and credit card benefits, here's what has changed from July 1.

Why India's energy scare may be ending sooner than expectedFuel prices have started easing in India as crude oil retreats, Nayara cuts pump rates and commercial LPG gets cheaper. The shift points to improving supplies after the West Asia conflict, though volatility remains.

Market open higher as auto stocks lead; monsoon worries keep gains in checkIndian benchmark indices opened higher as auto stocks led a recovery from Tuesday's losses. Gains stayed limited as monsoon concerns and stalled US-Iran talks kept investors cautious.

Gold prices fall, but buyers disappear as demand drops 70%Gold demand in India has fallen by over 70%, as higher import duty, subdued buying sentiment and a sharp correction in prices keep consumers away. With MCX gold down over Rs 50,000 from its record high, here's why demand has slumped and what experts expect next.

Nayara Energy cuts petrol price by Rs 5, diesel by Rs 3 as global oil rates coolNayara Energy has cut petrol prices by Rs 5 per litre and diesel by Rs 3 per litre across its nationwide retail network, becoming the first fuel retailer to reduce prices in over two years. The move follows a decline in global crude oil prices after easing tensions in West Asia.

Insurance mis-selling: IRDAI wants every policy linked to person who sold itIRDAI has proposed tagging every insurance policy to the individual who sold it instead of only the bank or intermediary. The move aims to improve accountability in insurance mis-selling cases and strengthen consumer protection.

Can you claim your EPF pension before 58? Here's what every employee must knowEarly retirement is becoming increasingly common, but what happens to your EPF pension if you stop working before 58? Can you start receiving it right away? Let us find out.

Price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinder reduced by Rs 183. Check new ratesThe government has slashed the price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinders by Rs 183 in a big relief to customers after multiple hikes due to the West Asia crisis.

Trump earned over $1.4 billion from crypto ventures in 2025, filings showA law from 1978 mandates the President and the Vice President of the US to declare their income as well as their assets. Trump's latest filings detailed the fact that his companies received almost USD 800 million from World Liberty Financial, a crypto venture he and his sons co-founded.

Centre hikes special duty on petrol, cuts levy on diesel, aviation fuel exportsThe Centre has raised the windfall tax on petrol exports while cutting levies on diesel and ATF from July 1. The revision also widens export duty exemptions and leaves domestic fuel excise rates unchanged.

RBI says banks' bad loans hit multi-decade low of 1.8%, warns of AI cyberattacksDespite repeated shocks, the global financial system has thus far demonstrated notable resilience, with markets remaining orderly after an initial bout of volatility following the outbreak of the West Asia conflict, the RBI said in its Financial Stability Report (FSR).

Kotak Mahindra to buy Deutsche Bank's retail business in India for $30 millionThe acquisition will help Kotak expand its presence among retail and affluent customers, while Deutsche Bank will focus on its core business areas.

UK says it's minded to intervene in $110-billion Paramount-Warner Bros mergerUK Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy said the proposed merger could have implications for Britain's media landscape and that she was considering intervention on public interest grounds.

Govt extends customs duty relief on key petrochemical imports until July 15Although the situation in West Asia has started to improve, the government has chosen to continue the customs duty exemption for a short period to help industries adjust without facing sudden cost pressures.

Noel Tata to step down as Voltas Chairman, announces decision at AGMDuring the AGM, Noel Tata said the company has built a strong leadership team by promoting talent from within and bringing in experienced professionals from outside.
Govt extends CBDT chairman Ravi Agrawal's term by six monthsRavi Agrawal's new term will begin on July 1, 2026, and continue till December 31, 2026, or until further orders, whichever comes earlier.

entertainment.headline: Diljit Dosanjh recalls performing at weddings for Rs 5,000 before stardom Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh has reflected on his financial struggles before fame, revealing he accepted wedding and birthday party gigs for as little as Rs 5,000. The singer-actor also recalled growing up in poverty, saying his family often couldn't afford medical treatment.

Bigg Boss 19's Tanya Mittal hits back after being called Shalini Passi's 'sasti copy' Tanya Mittal hit back after a journalist called her a 'sasti copy' of Shalini Passi. She said her decade-long journey was built through struggle and asked the media to report facts, not insults.

Toxic promo X review: Internet slams Yash's objectification of 'Ladies & Ladies' The new Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups promo, Ladies & Ladies, has sparked reactions on X, with several users criticising its portrayal of women. Many also questioned why the film's female stars had only brief appearances despite the promo's title.

Want to be free again: Angelina Jolie hasn't dated since divorce from Brad Pitt Actor Angelina Jolie said she has not dated since her divorce from Brad Pitt in 2016. While promoting her upcoming film Coutoure, she expressed her feelings about finding love.

Forget Tiger, Pathaan and Kabir. Alia Bhatt's Alpha could rewire YRF Spy Universe Alpha isn't just another addition to the YRF Spy Universe. Here's how Alia Bhatt's Sita transforms the franchise from patriotic spy-thriller into a darker, psychological story about an assassin, survival, trauma and identity.

How did Anushka's blue dress get longer in Sophie Dhawan's pic? Internet puzzled Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli were clicked at Lord's watching India play Australia. A new photo from the stands drew attention to the actor's dress and prompted comparisons online.

Akshay bows to Farida Jalal, Kiran Kumar in heartfelt Welcome To The Jungle note Akshay Kumar shared a gratitude note with a set photo featuring Farida Jalal and Kiran Kumar. The post came as Welcome To The Jungle crossed the original Welcome's lifetime worldwide gross in five days.

Inside Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's rumoured Thursday rehearsal dinner Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce could hold a rehearsal dinner on Thursday and marry on Friday at Madison Square Garden. The claimed plans, permits and security preparations have intensified fan speculation as the couple remain silent.

Ladies & Ladies: Yash's Toxic puts its women front and centre in new promo The makers of Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups have unveiled the new teaser Ladies & Ladies, shifting the focus to the film's women. Besides Yash, it stars Nayanthara, Tara Sutaria, Rukmini Vasanth, Kiara Advani and Huma Qureshi.

Emraan Hashmi overwhelmed by fans' love for Awarapan 2: 19 years of carrying Shivam Actor Emraan Hashmi thanked fans for the overwhelming response to the Awarapan 2 teaser. He said he has carried Shivam Pandit with him for the past 19 years. The sequel to the 2007 cult classic is scheduled to release on August 14, 2026.

This monsoon, Vikrant Massey finds love at Musafir Cafe; Netflix show to stream soon Netflix will stream Musafir Cafe from July 24, following three strangers whose lives intersect in Bhopal and Mussoorie. The romantic drama explores how chance encounters, love and loss reshape their journeys.

Hero hai, you can't say anything: Ssunita Ahuja on Govinda's extra-marital affairs On Lock Upp 2, Ssunita Ahuja spoke to Shreya Kalra about Govinda's alleged extra-marital affairs and said she chooses not to dwell on them. Her remarks add to her earlier public comments on their marriage, while Govinda has denied the allegations as a conspiracy.

Gaurav Khanna was unaware of Akanksha's Lock Upp split reveal: Actor Anuj Sachdeva Anuj Sachdeva said Gaurav Khanna did not know Akanksha Chamola would speak about their marriage on Lock Upp. He also defended Gaurav's silence and questioned why the disclosure was made publicly now.

Lock Upp former host Kangana Ranaut set to return to Season 2 as 'Janta Ki Awaaz' Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut is set to return to Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa this weekend as the special guest. Her appearance comes days after the reality show's revamped second season premiered with Farah Khan and Riteish Deshmukh as hosts.

Lock Upp 2: Influencer Shreya Kalra claims Akanksha Chamola is bisexual The latest episode of Lock Upp 2 saw another personal revelation involving contestant Akanksha Chamola. Days after Akanksha announcing separation from actor Gaurav Khanna, fellow contestant Shreya Kalra claimed to reveal her "secret" during the show.

Ahead of Aamir Khan's wedding, brother Faissal apologises to him, mother after feud Days before Aamir Khan's wedding to Gauri Spratt, the actor's brother Faissal Khan has apologised for the remarks he made against the family in public. He said rebuilding relationships now matters more to him than proving who was right or wrong.

Lock Upp 2: Ram Kapoor's 'I won't change' attitude leaves Farah Khan exasperated Filmmaker and host Farah Khan's confrontation with actor Ram Kapoor turned into a prickly on-air showdown on Lock Upp 2. His refusal to change his rude behaviour, even while being saved, became the episode's real spectacle.

SS Rajamouli shares big update on Varanasi, says 80-day shoot still pending Director SS Rajamouli said in France that Varanasi has about 80 days of shooting left. The update indicates the Mahesh Babu-led film remains on course for its grand April 2027 release.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 5: Film beats 2007 original's lifetime haul Welcome To The Jungle has crossed the lifetime worldwide gross of the 2007 original Welcome within five days of release. The Akshay Kumar-led comedy has earned Rs 120.12 crore worldwide, overtaking the first film's Rs 117.91 crore lifetime haul.

general news.headline: Brain, heart, lungs missing from body of Indian sailor returned by VenezuelaRakesh Chauhan died while on duty on a ship in Venezuela, and his body reached Deoria nearly a month later. A second post-mortem ordered by the district magistrate found all the organs missing, prompting the family to seek an investigation and compensation.

Bilawal Bhutto cites nuclear armageddon as Pak escalates Indus waters rowBilawal Bhutto-Zardari said any move to restrict Pakistan's Indus waters must be treated as a national security threat. He linked the treaty dispute to Pakistan's survival and warned that Islamabad would defend its water rights.

An open letter from 117 eminent India and Pak citizens to PM Modi, SharifMore than 100 eminent figures from India and Pakistan have urged PM Modi and Pakistani counterpart Shehbaz Sharif to restore dialogue and normal bilateral ties, including reinstating High Commissioners, resuming visa services and reopening airspace.

Annamalai doubles down on 'DMK Files' charges despite suit withdrawalK Annamalai said DMK MP TR Baalu has withdrawn the Rs 100 crore defamation case linked to his DMK Files allegations. He said he stands by every claim and noted the development comes as his 'We the Leaders' movement moves towards becoming a political party.

10 to 6 in office, AC bus and Hindu funds: How CM Vijay silenced his criticsFrom the day he entered politics, C Joseph Vijay was dismissed by his critics as inexperienced and unfit to govern. Yet, Tamil Nadu voted him to power. Just as his films opened to blockbuster success, his first moves as the chief minister have won him praise and silenced his critics.

Take my phone to Delhi, never to Beijing: US Senator's vote of trust in IndiaUS Senator Steve Daines said he leaves his phone behind when visiting China but carries it to India, using the contrast to highlight trust between the two democracies. He urged Washington to deepen ties with New Delhi to counter China's growing influence and innovation ecosystem.

Too fat for me: Karnataka man kills wife after years of body-shaming, abuseA staff nurse was allegedly strangled by her husband in Dharwad after months of reported abuse and dowry harassment. The case has brought renewed focus on allegations of body shaming, domestic violence and dowry demands within the marriage.

Hid money in washrooms: What Ram Mandir donation theft accused told copsAfter receiving court permission, police interrogated Avinash Shukla for nearly two hours on Tuesday, during which he admitted to stealing crores of rupees and described the alleged sequence of events.

Thief in PM Modi mask steals phones from Rajasthan shop, caught on cameraCCTV footage showed one of the burglars who targeted a mobile phone shop in Rajasthan's Bhilwara wearing a mask with Prime Minister Narendra Modi's face printed on it.

Why thousands of Bangladeshis are rushing to Indian visa centresIndia's resumption of tourist visas triggered long queues and over 1.4 lakh applications across Bangladesh. The rush underlines how geography, healthcare access and family ties continue to outweigh geopolitical strain.

Siya Goyal had Ketan's phone after death, cops probe possible tamperingPolice are examining whether Ketan Agarwal's phone was tampered with after his death. The finding could sharpen the conspiracy case against his fiancée Siya Goyal and her boyfriend Chetan Chaudhary.

Indian techie, 33, killed after car gets swept away in Kansas flash floodsA 33-year-old Indian national, Venkatesh Doppalapudi, died after his vehicle was swept away by severe flash flooding in the US state of Kansas.

Can India guarantee the right to walk without taking away someone's right to earn?The Supreme Court's footpath ruling has revived a conflict between pedestrians seeking safe pavements and vendors dependent on them for work.

IAS officer arrested on retirement day in Haryana's Rs 169-crore banking fraud caseThe CBI has arrested IAS officer Pardeep Kumar over the alleged siphoning of Rs 169 crore from Haryana State Pollution Control Board funds meant for fixed deposits. The case is part of a wider Rs 504-crore banking fraud involving multiple Haryana government departments and linked investigations in Chandigarh.

Siya told family she had ended friendship with Chetan after engagement: CopsPolice said Ketan Agarwal had raised concerns about his fiancee Siya Goyal before he was allegedly pushed off Lohagad Fort. The probe is now focused on deleted chats, reconstructed movements and whether others were involved in the alleged conspiracy.

The Kylian Mbappe magic that made France's bereaved coach Deschamps smileKylian Mbappe's first instinct after scoring for France was not to celebrate with the crowd, but to run to Didier Deschamps. The emotional moment came just four days after the France coach returned to the World Cup camp following his mother's funeral.

Why is Delhi targeting bikes first in its EV drive?The Delhi government has announced its electric vehicle (EV) policy that will stop registration of petrol two-wheelers from April 1, 2028. Why is the policy focusing on two-wheelers and three-wheelers first? Here's all you need to know.

UP hospital woman employee kicks patient 10 times in 35 seconds; suspendedThe clip, which sparked massive outrage on social media, showed a woman employee at a district hospital in Uttar Pradesh's Raebareli kicking a patient around 10 times while verbally abusing him.

Why US H-1B holders are turning to UK amid visa uncertainty, Green Card delaysAs US Green Card queues stretch into decades and Donald Trump's immigration policy grows more unpredictable, Indian H-1B professionals are looking beyond America. For many, Britain's Global Talent Visa has emerged as a faster, more certain pathway to permanent residency.

Vijay government approaches Supreme Court against complete cow slaughter banThe Tamil Nadu government has approached the Supreme Court challenging a Madras High Court order imposing a blanket ban on cow slaughter, arguing that the High Court's directive goes beyond the legal framework and seeks relief.

In the name of Rama: Fresh Ramayana spins face box office testRamayana, Varanasi and Alpha aim to recast elements of the epic. But recent experiments with the epic have strived to find takers among a deeply reverential audience.

How much rain must July bring to erase India's monsoon deficit?India's monsoon opened with a nearly 40 per cent rainfall deficit in June. Know what the IMD says about how much rain July will need to deliver to erase the deficit.

Meet the IITian behind Meowmbai: How a 25-year-old built Mumbai's viral cat mapAt 25, IIT Guwahati graduate Sudarshan Sudarshan built Meowmbai, a website mapping Mumbai's street cats. In this interview, he shares how curiosity, AI and product thinking transformed a personal passion project into a growing community platform with plans to support animal rescue, adoptions and, perhaps one day, lost pets.

Amazon sued for Prime Video ads, charging extra to remove themAmazon is facing legal trouble in Australia after introducing ads to Prime Video and asking subscribers to pay extra for an ad-free experience.

sports.headline: The Vikings are coming: Haaland stands in the way of Brazil in FIFA World Cup Erling Haaland's winning goal sent Norway into the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The team will now face 5-time champions Brazil in their second knockout fixture.

How the relentless domestic circuit is shaping Indian athletics Indian athletics is entering a new era where reputation offers no guarantees and domestic competition has become as ruthless as the global stage. At the Inter-State Championships in Bhubaneswar, established stars were pushed aside as emerging talent turned qualification battles into career-defining contests.

ICC rankings: Ishan Kishan replaces Abhishek Sharma as the new No. 1 T20I batter Ishan Kishan has climbed to the top of the ICC Men's T20I batting rankings for the first time, replacing teammate Abhishek Sharma to become the world's No. 1 batter in the shortest format.

Watch: Jubilant fans take over streets of Mexico after historic World Cup win The celebrations began inside the Azteca Stadium and quickly spilled onto the streets as Mexico ended a 40-year wait for a FIFA World Cup knockout victory. Fans across Mexico City erupted in celebration after El Tri booked their place in the Round of 16.

After embarrassing Ireland loss, did Shreyas Iyer snap at reporter? India are set for a quick turnover from the Ireland series as they take on England, starting July 1. Ahead of the opening game in Durham, fans have hit out at newly-appointed captain Shreyas Iyer for allegedly losing his cool against a journalist.

Jofra Archer tipped to test red-hot Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in England T20Is Former England spinner Graeme Swann believes England will struggle to contain Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the upcoming T20I series, although he feels Jofra Archer could prove to be the teenager's toughest test.

Mexico secure first knockout win in 40 years, oust Ecuador to reach last 16 Azteca was rocking long before kick-off and the celebrations continued well after the final whistle as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 to register their first FIFA World Cup knockout victory in 40 years and book a place in the last 16.

Another T20I. Same question. Will India hand Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut? England vs India, 1st T20I preview: As Shreyas Iyer's wounded India look to bounce back from a shock series defeat in Ireland, a powerhouse England side awaits in Durham with the clamour growing to hand 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut.

Serena Williams faces backlash after cancelling press conference at Wimbledon Serena Williams came under fire on social media after cancelling her post-match press conference following a heartbreaking first-round exit at The Championships, Wimbledon on Tuesday.

Mbappe prioritises titles over Lionel Messi's all-time scoring record Kylian Mbappe brushed aside talk of Lionel Messi's World Cup scoring record after France's win over Sweden, insisting his only focus is leading Les Bleus to the FIFA World Cup final on July 19.

Henry explains why Olise, not Mbappe, is France's most important player France great Thierry Henry believes Michael Olise has become Les Bleus' most important player after the Bayern Munich star starred in their 3-0 win over Sweden in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32. Kylian Mbappe scored twice to send France into the last 16, but Olise's two assists and all-round display stole the show.

Emotional Wawrinka fights back tears after final Wimbledon farewellWimbledon: Stan Wawrinka bid an emotional farewell after a battling four-set loss to Matteo Berrettini, fighting back tears as the crowd rose to applaud the three-time Grand Slam champion in his final appearance at the All England Club.

MEX 2-0 ECU Highlights: Clinical Mexico reach R16, Ecuador knocked outMexico (MEX) vs Ecuador (ECU) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico have won their first knockout game in 40 years. They beat Ecuador 2-0 in the R32 match and qualify for the next round without conceding a single goal. Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez's goals are enough for the co-hosts.

Koeman calls time on Netherlands managerial tenure after R32 World Cup exitRonald Koeman has stepped down as the Netherlands head coach after the Dutch were knocked out by Morocco in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, ending his second spell in charge after a disappointing campaign.

Mbappe makes World Cup knockouts his own as France crush Sweden in Round of 32FIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe scored twice to extend his World Cup knockout scoring record as France beat Sweden 3-0 to reach the Round of 16, setting up a clash with Paraguay while moving within one goal of Lionel Messi's all-time tally.

Serena Williams loses on Wimbledon return, but daughter sees a champion's gritSerena Williams' long-awaited Grand Slam return ended in a three-set loss to Maya Joint, but the 23-time major champion reminded Centre Court of her trademark resilience before turning her focus to doubles.

'What's the point of VAR?' Drogba fumes after Ivory Coast's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup 2026: Didier Drogba blasted the officials after Ivory Coast were denied a penalty in their 2-1 World Cup defeat to Norway. The VAR call overshadowed Erling Haaland's late winner and Ivory Coast's exit.

FRA 3-0 SWE Highlights: Mbappe brace powers France through to Round of 16France (FRA) vs Sweden (SWE) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (FRA 3-0 SWE) After a barrage of chances by France to open the scoring, Kylian Mbappe finally opens the scoring at the stroke of half-time. Barcola doubled the lead in the second half. Mbappe makes it a brace as France completes a rout of Sweden in New York.

Haaland has last laugh as Norway spoil Diallo's dazzling display for Ivory CoastFIFA World Cup 2026: Amad Diallo's sensational cameo looked set to inspire Ivory Coast to a famous comeback before Erling Haaland struck late to send Norway into the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16. Norway will now face five-time champions Brazil in a blockbuster knockout clash.

Ashleigh Gardner stars as Australia reaches yet another World Cup finalWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Australia overwhelmed West Indies at The Oval to reach another Women's T20 World Cup final. Beth Mooney and Ashleigh Gardner turned a semi-final into routine, reinforcing Australia's knockout dominance.

LeBron James leaves Lakers after eight seasons, set to test NBA free agencyLeBron James has informed the Lakers that he will continue his NBA career elsewhere, ending an eight-year spell in Los Angeles as the Warriors, Heat and Cavaliers emerge as potential destinations.

Shelton crashes out after five-set thriller as Swiatek scrapes into next roundWimbledon 2026: Otto Virtanen stunned Ben Shelton in a five-set Wimbledon first-round epic, while Iga Swiatek survived Taylor Townsend. The Finn claimed a career-best win as the champion exposed nerves but advanced.

CIV 1-2 NOR Highlights: Erling Haaland sends Norway throughCote d'Ivoire (CIV) vs Norway (NOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (CIV 1-2 NOR) Norway survived a fierce Ivory Coast fightback to punch their ticket to the Round of 16. Now comes the ultimate test as Antonio Nusa, Erling Haaland and company prepare to take on five-time champions Brazil.

Not just Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Shreyas Iyer says India seniors deserved backingENG vs IND: Shreyas Iyer said India's World Cup-winning seniors deserve continued backing ahead of the England T20I series. His remarks came as calls grow for teenager Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to make his long-awaited India debut.

Day 2 Highlights: Serena Williams goes down fighting in comeback gameWimbledon 2026 Day 2 Highlights: Maya Joint spoilt Serena Williams' long-awaited return to professional tennis at Wimbledon, defeating the 23-time Grand Slam champion 6-3, 6-7(6-8), 6-3 in a gripping three-set contest. After Williams battled back to force a decider, the 20-year-old Australian kept her composure in the final set to complete one of the biggest wins of her career.

Hardik Pandya shifts to Bengaluru, makes BCCI facility permanent training baseHardik Pandya has relocated to Bengaluru to train regularly at the BCCI's Centre of Excellence. The move marks an unusual long-term shift as he continues rehabilitation from injury and plans his white-ball future.

Ben Stokes didn't inform Brendon McCullum of retirement until last minuteBen Stokes reportedly told Brendon McCullum about his retirement only on Sunday morning, moments before the England squad was informed. The all-rounder announced his retirement midway through a Test match against England, which ended on Monday. India T

What's wrong with Sooryavanshi carrying drinks? R Ashwin hits back at criticsThe Indian team was criticised for not handing 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his T20I debut against Ireland. Ahead of the 5-match England series, Ashwin asks, why is it so bad that the youngster was made to carry drinks during the tour.

Shashank Singh denies assault allegations: Cook was taking private videosPunjab Kings cricketer Shashank Singh has denied allegations of assault and wrongful confinement made by his former cook. A day after an FIR was registered against him and his father in Bhopal, Shashank responded to the allegations publicly.

Day 2 schedule: Serena Williams returns to Centre Court, centre stageSerena Williams returns to Centre Court against Maya Joint as Wimbledon's Day 2 schedule takes shape. Her comeback headlines a packed slate featuring Iga Swiatek, Alexander Zverev and Elena Rybakina.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
